text: It is difficult to see how this verse fits into its surroundings. Some would translate ‘What is it that makes its way up …’ and treat verse 7 as the answer; but the analogy of 8.5 suggests that the reference is somehow to the heroine of the poem.
vv. 7-11: These verses are plainly an interlude, in the form of a song (perhaps chanted by the women of Jerusalem) in honour of king Solomon’s state litter.
Literally, ‘Within, it was inlaid with love, on account of (in the Hebrew text, from) the daughters of Jerusalem’.
